headline: Starbucks shareholders elected all 11 director nominees, approved executive compensation, and passed a shareholder proposal to replace supermajority voting with majority voting at the 2026 Annual Meeting.
investor_impact: The transition from supermajority to majority voting improves corporate governance and strengthens shareholder rights, aligning with broader ESG best practices. The routine election of the board and overwhelming rejection of fringe shareholder proposals indicate stable governance with no immediate operational or financial shifts to the investment thesis.
key_figures: 11 directors elected to serve until the 2027 Annual Meeting
774,932,476 votes FOR advisory resolution on executive compensation
Deloitte & Touche LLP ratified as independent auditor for FY 2026
823,985,324 votes FOR replacing supermajority voting with majority voting requirements
